expected_behavior: The valid promotion fixture parses, the bad record-only mutation fixture fails, the proposal payload parses, and a mismatched promotion payload fails.
failure_mode: Promotion records can claim target mutation or machine-applicable content without contract enforcement.

expected_behavior: A promotion for an approved proposal stores under .krn/promotions without mutating the proposal target in record-only mode.
failure_mode: KRN records promotion-looking state without approval, append-only persistence, or target-mutation boundary.

expected_behavior: Explicit apply mode writes exact memory payload content only after an approved review decision.
failure_mode: Promotion writes inferred or unapproved content instead of exact reviewed payload content.

expected_behavior: Explicit apply mode writes exact init agent-instructions payload content only after an approved review decision.
assertions: AGENTS.md written in explicit apply mode
failure_mode: KRN init can skip the review/promotion spine and write AGENTS.md without an exact approved payload.

expected_behavior: Explicit apply mode writes exact init local-config payload content only after an approved review decision.
failure_mode: KRN init can skip the review/promotion spine and write .krn/config.toml without an exact approved payload.

expected_behavior: Explicit apply mode writes exact init source-pointers payload content only after an approved review decision.
failure_mode: KRN init can skip the review/promotion spine and write .krn/sources/index.json without an exact approved payload.

expected_behavior: Explicit apply mode writes exact init context-pointers payload content only after an approved review decision.
assertions: context pointer index keeps memory bodies out of runtime seed
failure_mode: KRN init can skip the review/promotion spine and write .krn/context/index.json without an exact approved payload or with a runtime seed that stores memory bodies.

expected_behavior: Explicit apply mode writes exact init eval-baseline payload content only after an approved review decision.
assertions: eval baseline keeps lab/all and lift claims out of default bootstrap
failure_mode: KRN init can skip the review/promotion spine and write .krn/evals/baseline.json without an exact approved payload or with lab/all defaults or productivity-lift claims in the seed.

expected_behavior: A rejected proposal review decision cannot be promoted.
failure_mode: Rejected proposals can still write promotion records or target files.

expected_behavior: An approved proposal without machine-applicable payload cannot be promoted.
failure_mode: KRN infers target file content from prose instead of requiring a typed payload.

expected_behavior: Storing the same promotion twice returns the existing promotion path without duplicate records.
failure_mode: Promotion persistence duplicates promotion records and loses idempotency.

expected_behavior: A promotion with a target path outside the target root is rejected before persistence or target writes.
failure_mode: Promotion weakens the target-path safety model after review approval.
